summary: This extremely brief post directs readers to a Bloomberg article featuring two graphs about wealth and race. O'Neil lets the data visualizations speak for themselves rather than providing her own analysis. The post implies the graphs are self-evidently powerful in illustrating racial disparities in wealth accumulation. By describing the article as 'amazing' and the graphs as 'fantastic,' she signals strong endorsement of the piece's findings.
key_points: Bloomberg published compelling data visualizations about race and wealth
The graphs are presented as self-evident proof requiring little commentary
The title connects racial inequality to 'Race to the Top' education policy rhetoric
money_quotes: Bloomberg has a pretty amazing article today with two fantastic graphs.
The graphs pretty much say it all.
key_insight: Sometimes data visualizations are so powerful they require no additional commentary to make their point about racial inequality.
